stimulus.content: George Washington led the Continental Army during the American Revolution. After the war, he was chosen to lead the Constitutional Convention in 1787. In 1789, he became the first President of the United States.

stem: What role did George Washington play at the Constitutional Convention?

choices.text: He wrote the entire Constitution alone
He led the convention as its president
He refused to attend the meeting
He argued against creating a new plan

answer_explanation: The passage states Washington was chosen to lead the Constitutional Convention. He served as its president. He did not write it alone, refuse to attend, or argue against a new plan.
